name: Manufacturing
description: Industrial AI governance for safety-critical systems, autonomous machinery, predictive maintenance, and quality control.

highRiskCategories.category: AI as safety component of machinery
highRiskCategories.description: AI systems intended to be used as a safety component of a product, or which are themselves a product, covered by EU harmonisation legislation including the Machinery Regulation, Radio Equipment Directive, and Pressure Equipment Directive.
AI systems intended to be used as safety components in the management and operation of critical digital infrastructure, road traffic, or in the supply of water, gas, heating, and electricity.

sectorRequirements.requirement: AI systems used as safety components of machinery or products must comply with product safety conformity assessments under the EU Machinery Regulation and relevant harmonized standards.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 6(1), Annex I; EU Machinery Regulation 2023/1230; ISO 12100; Malaysia OSHA 1994

sectorRequirements.requirement: Implement functional safety requirements for AI-controlled autonomous systems including emergency stop mechanisms, fail-safe modes, and defined safety integrity levels.
sectorRequirements.source: IEC 61508; ISO 13849; EU Machinery Regulation; NIST AI RMF MAP function; Malaysia DOSH Guidelines

sectorRequirements.requirement: Maintain detailed technical documentation for AI systems embedded in industrial equipment, including training data specifications, design choices, performance metrics, and known limitations.
sectorRequirements.source: EU AI Act Art. 11; ISO 42001 Annex A; NIST AI RMF GOVERN function

sectorRequirements.requirement: Conduct ongoing reliability and performance monitoring of predictive maintenance AI systems, with documented validation against actual equipment failure data and calibration requirements.
sectorRequirements.source: ISO 42001 Clause 9; NIST AI RMF MEASURE function; EU AI Act Art. 72; Malaysia NGAIGE Reliability Principle

sectorRequirements.requirement: Ensure cybersecurity protections for AI-controlled industrial systems including network segmentation, adversarial robustness testing, and secure update mechanisms for deployed models.
sectorRequirements.source: NIST AI RMF MANAGE function; IEC 62443; EU Cyber Resilience Act; Malaysia NGAIGE Safety Principle

sectorRequirements.requirement: Implement worker safety governance for human-robot collaboration scenarios, including risk assessments for AI-driven cobots, autonomous guided vehicles, and automated quality inspection systems.
sectorRequirements.source: EU Machinery Regulation; ISO 10218; ISO/TS 15066; OSHA Guidelines; Malaysia OSHA 1994

sectorRequirements.requirement: Assess environmental and societal impacts of AI-driven manufacturing optimization, including energy consumption, workforce displacement, and supply chain sustainability implications.
sectorRequirements.source: OECD AI Principles Sustainable Development; UNESCO AI Ethics; Malaysia NGAIGE Sustainability; EU AI Act Recital 27

keyRisks.description: AI-controlled machinery or autonomous systems may fail in unexpected ways under novel conditions not represented in training data, potentially causing physical harm.
AI systems controlling manufacturing processes may be vulnerable to adversarial manipulation, leading to quality defects, safety hazards, or production sabotage.
Excessive reliance on AI predictive maintenance may lead to neglect of traditional inspection practices, missing failure modes not captured in historical data.
AI-driven collaborative robots may misjudge worker positions or intentions, creating collision and injury risks in shared workspaces.
AI-optimized just-in-time supply chains may amplify disruptions when models fail to predict or adapt to unprecedented supply shocks.
